widget_id: Name of the SURVIVING Foreign Entity in the Jurisdiction of Organization
label_verbatim: FIRST: Name of the SURVIVING Foreign Entity in the Jurisdiction of Organization:
rationale: Surviving entity's name in its home (non-Maine) jurisdiction. Uses the entity-name-vs-home-jurisdiction convention's home_jurisdiction_name primitive scoped under merger.surviving_entity.
State or country where the surviving entity is organized.

widget_id: If authorized to transact business in Maine the date authorized is
label_verbatim: If authorized to transact business in Maine, the date authorized is:
rationale: Date the surviving entity was qualified/registered in Maine, if applicable. Blank if survivor was not previously authorized in Maine.

widget_id: New name of surviving foreign entity if changed as result of merger
label_verbatim: New name of surviving foreign entity, if changed as result of merger:
rationale: Post-merger home name of the surviving entity, if the merger changed it. Blank means name unchanged.

label_verbatim: SECOND: Name of the NONSURVIVING Foreign Entity in the Jurisdiction of Organization (attach additional pages if necessary):
rationale: Nonsurviving (merged-away) entity's home name. Field-id is the truncated tail of the label ('attach additional pages if necessary'), an artifact of the template's auto-named widget. The 'attach additional pages' language permits multiple nonsurviving entities to be listed via attachments — only one inline slot is bound.
Home jurisdiction of the nonsurviving entity. Field-id is auto-suffixed '_2' to disambiguate from the survivor's identical label.

widget_id: Date authorized to transact business in Maine if applicable
label_verbatim: Date authorized to transact business in Maine (if applicable):
rationale: Date the nonsurviving entity was qualified/registered in Maine, if applicable. Blank means it was never authorized in Maine.

label_verbatim: Hold attested copy for pick up
